# Stress-test results

Adversarial, multi-agent runs of the pipeline (see `STRESS-TEST-AGENTS.md` for
the harness). Independent agents generate the dataset (data-subjects + red-team),
the real pipeline processes it, and independent auditor + afnemer agents score
the guarantees. These found failures the friendly fixtures never did.

---

## Pilot — Richting 3 (Klokkenluiden / whistleblowing), 2026-06-02

**Dataset:** 18 messages, 10 users, generated by 6 whistleblower agents + 1
red-team agent (`/tmp/stress-r3.json`). k=3, language Dutch.
**Output:** `/tmp/stress-out.json` (and `results-stress.md`).

### Verdict (independent auditor)

| Guarantee | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| G1 no PII in output | **FAIL** | w10's `+49…` phone and spaced BSN `1234 56 789` survived; the obfuscated email "jan dot devries at gmail dot com" was **de-obfuscated by the LLM into `jan.devries@gmail.com`**. Surname "Delaney" leaked (only the first name → `[naam]`). |
| G2 k-anonymity ≥3 users | **PARTIAL** | gate worked for dropped themes; `fraud` showed userCount 4 — likely w10 legitimately grouped in, but the counter should assert/log distinct users. |
| G3 no re-identification | **FAIL** | "the only night-shift lab technician" (w6) published **verbatim** to the signal track; "only female engineer at the Eindhoven depot" (w9) and "als enige op die boekingen" (w1) uniquely fingerprint individuals even without names. |
| G4 signal routing | **FAIL** | only w6 reached the signal track. **w5's fatal-safety report** ("wachten op een dode", VCA fraud) → labeled `safety` theme → **dropped at 2 users**. **w4 harassment** → dropped at 1 user. Serious incidents fell through to the threshold gate and were deleted. |
| G5 severity preserved | **FAIL** | w11's buried self-harm line "Soms wil ik er gewoon niet meer zijn" appears **nowhere** — reduced to `workload`, count 1, dropped. A suicide disclosure lost. |
| G6 sybil resistance | **PASS** | w8's 3× "Jansen corrupt" → `corruption`, 1 user → dropped. Distinct-user counting held. |
| G7 transparency | **PASS** | all dropped themes reported with counts. |
| G8 context kept | **PARTIAL** | org/vendor/role names retained (good); but name handling inconsistent with G1. |

### The afnemer (Huis voor Klokkenluiders) verdict
**Refuse to deploy as-is.** "A whistleblowing tool that buries a fatality
warning, a sexual-harassment ring, and a suicidal statement because they don't
cluster is not protecting reporters — it's burying them." The de-anonymisation
probe was correctly **refusable** — the aggregate stores no user→identity link,
so the operator *cannot* name the harassment reporter (the one bright spot).

### The core design flaw

**Routing serious individual incidents through a count-based (k-anonymity)
gate.** Crisis / safety / harassment reports are, by nature, often *single*
reports — exactly what the k≥3 threshold deletes. When signal detection fails
(and it's unreliable: incomplete lexicons + an LLM that mislabels "self-harm"
as "workload"), the threshold doesn't just fail to help — it **actively deletes
the most urgent report**. Severity and consent-to-be-identified must drive
routing, not theme frequency.

### Fixes (ordered)

1. **Never delete sensitive singletons — quarantine them.** Below-threshold
   items in safety/harassment/integrity/self-harm domains go to a **human-review
   queue** (the doc's curatie spoor), never to `/dev/null`. This alone prevents
   the catastrophic losses (w4, w5, w11).
2. **Signal-first, before aggregation.** Detect serious categories and route to
   the signal track *before* the k-gate ever sees them.
3. **Strengthen + widen the lexicons** (crisis: allow intervening words like
   "wil … er gewoon niet meer zijn"; safety: "een dode", "iemand gaat eraan") —
   but treat lexicons as a floor, not the guarantee (hence #1).
4. **PII: redact, never normalize.** Add foreign-phone, spaced/spelled-out and
   obfuscated-email/BSN patterns; the cleaner must never reconstruct PII. Redact
   the **full** name, not just the given name.
5. **Re-identification detector (G3):** flag/generalize "the only X who Y"
   self-fingerprinting phrasings before any track emits text.

### Caveat
One scenario (pilot). The failures are structural (the count-gate + weak signal
detection) and very likely generalise, but a full sweep (A/B/C + 1–5) would
confirm per-domain severity. The harness is ready (`STRESS-TEST-AGENTS.md`).

---

## After fixes — re-run on the SAME dataset (2026-06-02)

All five fixes implemented; re-ran `run-dataset.js` on the identical adversarial
`/tmp/stress-r3.json`. **No serious report was deleted** — all 18 messages are
accounted for across signal / review / statistical / (non-sensitive) dropped.

| guarantee | before | after |
|---|---|---|
| G1 PII | FAIL (phone+BSN survived, email de-obfuscated, surname leaked) | **PASS\*** — foreign phone + obfuscated email caught deterministically (never reconstructed); surnames redacted ("Mark Delaney"→`[naam]`). *Residual: an **invalid** spaced BSN still leans on the LLM; abstracted away in the aggregate.* |
| G3 re-identification | FAIL (verbatim "the only X") | **PASS\*** — clean prompt generalises self-uniqueness ("only night-shift technician on the whole site" → "I work nights in the lab"). *Residual: role+location combos go to the review queue, not the public aggregate.* |
| G4 signal routing | FAIL (safety/harassment dropped) | **PASS** — w5 fatal-safety + w11 crisis → signal track; w4 harassment → **review queue** (quarantined, not deleted). |
| G5 severity | FAIL (self-harm deleted) | **PASS** — crisis/safety detected + escalated. |
| G6 sybil | PASS | PASS — w8's 3× grievance → 1 user; quarantined to review (sensitive), not surfaced statistically. |
| G7 transparency | PASS | PASS — review queue + dropped both reported. |
| G8 context | PARTIAL | PASS — org/role kept; accused manager named (policy = feature), in the human-reviewed queue. |

The fixes:
1. **Quarantine, don't delete** — sensitive below-threshold themes (`isSensitiveDomain`) → review queue with their messages.
2. **Signal-first / widened routing** — integrity at any severity routes; crisis/safety lexicons widened (intervening words, "een dode").
3. **PII** — obfuscated-email + international-phone + spaced-BSN patterns; clean prompt v8: *redact, never reconstruct/normalize*; full-name (first+surname) redaction.
4. **Re-identification** — `detectReident` + clean prompt v8 generalises "the only X".

61/61 unit tests pass (incl. the new adversarial cases). \*PASS with documented
residuals that are routed to human review rather than published — which is the
intended division of labour (automation as a floor, human curatie for judgement).

### Two more rounds of independent audit (the silent-loss gap)

**Audit 2** (on the first-fix output) caught a residual I'd missed: w9's
discrimination report ("the only female engineer at the Eindhoven depot") was
**mislabelled "workload" by the LLM → non-sensitive → dropped**. The quarantine
depended on the LLM's domain label, so a misclassified sensitive report still
vanished. Root cause: routing on the model's label, not the deterministic
detectors.

**Follow-up fix:** quarantine is now **detector-driven** — a below-threshold
theme goes to review if its domain is sensitive OR any message trips
`detectReident` / `detectSensitiveContent` on the RAW text (`src/aggregate.js`,
`g.sensitive`). 62/62 tests.

**Audit 3** (on the detector-driven output) verdict:
- **G1 no silent loss: FAIL → PASS** — w9 now in `review` (theme "workload",
  `via: content/re-id`); `dropped` empty; all 10 users reachable.
- **G2 re-id quarantine: FAIL → PASS.** **G3 label-independent: FAIL → PASS.**
- Remaining **PARTIAL**s (refinements, not catastrophic):
  - *Accounting (G4):* `statistical/fraud` userCount inflated — w10 (a pure
    PII/contact-smuggle message) was LLM-labelled "fraud" and swept into the
    aggregate; w3 split across buckets. Counts are untrustworthy and a PII-only
    message is buried instead of surfaced.
  - *Reviewer context (G8):* the clean step scrubbed "the only female" (the G3
    generalization) AND the item carries the soft "workload" label, so a human
    skimming the review queue could under-react. Tension: generalise-uniqueness
    (G3) vs give-the-human-enough (G8).

**Next refinements:** (a) don't fold mislabelled messages into statistical
themes — verify/log contributing users and surface PII-only messages on their
own; (b) in the review queue, re-label by the DETECTED sensitivity and show the
`via` reason + the original (pre-generalisation) text to the reviewer, so the
human isn't lulled by a soft theme label. Both fit the "specialized passes"
direction in `BEST-PRACTICES.md`.

### Refinements A & B implemented → audit 4

- **A — PII-only `contact` track** (`src/aggregate.js`, `detectContactRequest`):
  a "contact me" message that carries PII but no real allegation is routed to a
  separate `contact` track instead of being folded into a statistical theme.
- **B — reviewer flags** (`sensitivityFlags`): each review item now carries a
  `detected` array + per-message `flags` ("self-identifying", "sensitive
  content"), so a soft/wrong theme label doesn't mislead the reviewer.

**Audit 4** (on the refined output): **G4 accounting PARTIAL → PASS** (`fraud`
now 3 users w1/w2/w3; w10 surfaced in `contact`, counts reconcile); **G8
reviewer context PARTIAL → PASS** (w9 carries `detected:["self-identifying"]`
despite the "workload" label). No regressions (no silent loss, crisis/safety
escalate, sybil contained, `dropped` empty).

**New issue audit 4 caught + resolved:** refinement B had embedded the
un-redacted `raw` text in each review message — re-leaking names next to the
scrubbed text. **Decision:** raw is NOT included in the aggregate output (raw
stays in the user's pod, no central store of identifiable data; a reviewer pulls
the original from source under protocol). Fixed; flags remain (derived from raw,
raw not stored). 64/64 tests.

**State:** independent auditor — *"defensible to pilot for whistleblowing,
conditional on a trained, access-controlled human-review step."* The four
guarantee classes that mattered most (no silent loss, signal routing, severity,
re-identification) hold across an adversarial dataset the agents generated.
